name: Harness Cloud Cost Management (CCM)
description: Cloud cost management platform that gives DevOps/FinOps teams real-time visibility, automated optimization, and governance across cloud providers and Kubernetes.

details.targetMarket: FinOps, CloudOps, DevOps and engineering teams at mid-to-large enterprises running multi-cloud and Kubernetes environments

items: Kubernetes-native cost breakdown (namespaces, workloads, pods, nodes, labels)
Hourly cost granularity and detailed allocation for teams/services
Support for FinOps Open Cost & Usage (FOCUS) and custom cost categories
AI-powered rightsizing recommendations for VMs and containers
Cloud AutoStopping to automatically stop/start idle non-production resources
Commitment Orchestrator to manage RIs and Savings Plans
Cluster Orchestrator for EKS (spot orchestration / autoscaling)
Auto-inference of implemented recommendations and savings tracking
Cloud Asset Governance with Governance-as-Code and auto-remediation
Chargeback / showback reporting and business-perspective views
Integrations with Jira/ServiceNow for tracking recommendation implementation
Harness AIDA — AI assistant to generate policies and help analyze costs
Collaborative dashboards and shareable perspectives for engineering and finance
SaaS offering with a self-managed enterprise option (self-hosted CCM with AWS install requirement for full features),
APIs and integrations for cloud provider billing APIs, ticketing systems, and the wider Harness platform

missingFeatures: Transparent, entry-level per-seat or usage pricing — paid plans are enterprise/custom which can be a barrier for smaller orgs
Deeper out-of-the-box integrations with third-party observability/monitoring tools (customers request easier Datadog/Splunk/Prometheus linkages)
More mature CFO/accounting-focused features (invoice reconciliation, accounting exports) compared with finance-centric tooling
Self-hosted feature parity and deployment flexibility: self-managed CCM requires AWS for full functionality
Simpler packaged SKUs for customers who want bite-sized, single-purpose cost modules rather than bundled enterprise SKUs

pricingPlans.name: Free Forever
pricingPlans.note: Free plan for smaller customers (limited to organisations under certain cloud spend thresholds — documentation references <$250K/year limits) with core visibility and recommendations.

pricingPlans.name: Enterprise (CCM)
pricingPlans.note: Enterprise licensing with modular SKUs (can buy CCM SKUs individually or as a bundle). Required for customers with larger cloud spend or advanced automation/governance needs.

strengths: Strong automation-first approach (AutoStopping, Commitment & Cluster Orchestrators) that can actively reduce bills
Kubernetes-native cost visibility and per-workload granularity not offered by all competitors
Integrated with the broader Harness DevOps platform — useful for teams already using Harness CI/CD and feature flag modules
AI-driven recommendations and verification (auto-inference) speed up FinOps workflows
weaknesses: Pricing and packaging are enterprise-centric and can lack transparency for smaller buyers
May require additional integrations or tooling for full CFO-level finance workflows (invoice reconciliation, accounting exports)
Self-managed deployment has limitations (AWS-dependency for full feature parity) and can add operational overhead
